clauses.text: This Agreement is entered into between [CLIENT_NAME] ("Client") and [PROVIDER_NAME] ("Service Provider").
This Agreement governs the provision of [PROJECT_NAME] by the Service Provider to the Client.

The Service Provider shall develop the software according to the requirements documented in [REQUIREMENTS_DOCUMENT]. This document forms the contractual baseline for the Deliverables. Changes to [REQUIREMENTS_DOCUMENT] after approval require the Change Control mechanism in the Milestones Clause.
The Service Provider shall deliver the Deliverables to the Client on or before [DELIVERY_DATE].
The Services shall take place on [DELIVERY_DATE] at , subject to any change confirmed in writing by both Parties.
The Client shall pay the Service Provider [TOTAL_VALUE] for the Services, payable via [PAYMENT_METHOD] according to the schedule agreed between the Parties.
If the Client fails to pay any amount due by its due date, the Service Provider may charge interest on the overdue amount at [LATE_PAYMENT_RATE] per month.

The Client shall test each Deliverable against [REQUIREMENTS_DOCUMENT] through [ACCEPTANCE_TEST_PHASES] within [ACCEPTANCE_PERIOD_DAYS] days of delivery of that phase. If the Client does not respond within this period, that phase shall be deemed accepted.
Upon full payment of [TOTAL_VALUE], the Service Provider assigns to the Client all rights, title, and interest in the custom source code developed under this Agreement.
Where the Client requests ongoing maintenance following delivery, the Service Provider shall provide it for a fee agreed separately between the Parties.
Where the Client requires defined service levels for the software post-launch, the Parties shall agree service level targets under a separate Service Level Agreement.
Where the Services include training, the Service Provider shall provide [TRAINING_HOURS] hours of training on using the software.
Where the Client requests hosting, the Service Provider shall arrange it for a fee agreed separately between the Parties.
Where the software requires cloud infrastructure, the Service Provider shall provision it using [CLOUD_PROVIDER], with associated infrastructure costs billed separately to the Client.
Where the Client requests technical support beyond the Warranty Clause period, the Service Provider shall provide it at [SUPPORT_HOURLY_RATE] per hour.
The Service Provider shall deliver technical documentation covering [DOCUMENTATION_SCOPE] alongside the final Deliverable.

The business objectives underlying [REQUIREMENTS_DOCUMENT] are described in [BUSINESS_REQUIREMENTS_REFERENCE], which the Parties shall use to resolve ambiguity in interpreting the Requirements Clause.
The technical constraints underlying [REQUIREMENTS_DOCUMENT] are described in [TECHNICAL_REQUIREMENTS_REFERENCE].
The Service Provider shall document the proposed software architecture in [ARCHITECTURE_DOCUMENT] and obtain the Client's written approval before beginning implementation of that architecture.

The project shall proceed according to the milestones described in [MILESTONE_SCHEDULE]. A milestone is complete when its Deliverable passes the acceptance testing described in the Acceptance Testing Clause. Changes to [REQUIREMENTS_DOCUMENT] after a milestone's requirements are approved shall be treated as a change request requiring written agreement on any adjustment to [TOTAL_VALUE] or [DELIVERY_DATE].

The Service Provider warrants that the software shall be free of material defects for [WARRANTY_PERIOD_DAYS] days from final acceptance. Defects reported within this period shall be corrected at no additional charge.
Each Party retains ownership of intellectual property it owned before this Agreement, including any framework, library, or tool the Service Provider incorporates into the software.

Each Party shall keep confidential any non-public information disclosed by the other Party in connection with this Agreement and shall not disclose it without the disclosing Party's prior written consent.
Neither Party shall be liable to the other for any indirect, incidental, or consequential loss arising from this Agreement.
Each Party's total liability arising from this Agreement shall not exceed [TOTAL_VALUE], except for loss arising from gross negligence, willful misconduct, or fraud.

Neither Party shall be liable for a failure to perform its obligations to the extent that failure results from an event beyond that Party's reasonable control that could not have been avoided through reasonable diligence. The affected Party shall notify the other Party without undue delay.
This Agreement shall be governed by [APPLICABLE_LAW]. Any dispute shall be submitted to [DISPUTE_RESOLUTION_MECHANISM] before litigation, except where interim injunctive relief is required.

The Parties have executed this Agreement as of [SIGNATURE_DATE]. Ambiguity: none — Requirements Clause establishes [REQUIREMENTS_DOCUMENT] as the single contractual baseline, with Business/Technical Requirements Clauses referencing it rather than creating competing definitions. Duplication: none — Business Requirements and Technical Requirements address distinct concerns (why vs. how) and both point back to the same baseline document rather than duplicating its content. Loophole closed: Milestones Clause explicitly routes requirement changes through change control with adjustment to value/date, closing Step 2 risk #3 and #5 together. Contradiction: none. Missing protection: Architecture Clause requires written approval before implementation, preventing costly rebuilds from unapproved architectural decisions. Complexity: kept documentation-reference Clauses lightweight, pointing to external documents rather than embedding full technical content in Master English legal text. Enforceability: multi-phase acceptance testing explicitly tied to the same requirements baseline used throughout the Agreement. Attack: Client claims the delivered software doesn't match unwritten expectations — mitigated by Requirements Clause establishing a single documented baseline as the sole contractual standard. Attack: Provider changes the architecture mid-build without telling the Client, causing integration failures — mitigated by Architecture Clause's approval requirement. Attack: A milestone is marked "complete" by the Provider despite failing acceptance testing — mitigated by Milestones Clause explicitly defining completion as passing acceptance testing, not mere delivery. No further gaps found. Version 1.0.0 — Self-Review and Red Team passed — Pending Human Legal Review.
